steps.title: Identify the subject structure
steps.content:
The subject is Paul Austerlitz of Gettysburg College (singular).

The phrase along with researchers Maureen Mahon of New York University and Michael Veal of Yale University is a prepositional phrase.

Unlike a coordinating conjunction, along with doesn't make the subject plural.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
The verb must agree with the singular head noun of the subject. D (serves) is the only singular verb.

The others use plural verbs—are serving, have served, and serve—violating agreement with a singular subject.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
